justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Strong signals explicitly acknowledged — observability, load testing, rollback called out as genuinely strong multiple times. (2) Non-idempotent write + retry = guaranteed duplicates identified as THE blocker, called 'mathematical certainty.' (3) DLQ with no consumer identified as silent data loss risk — called 'write-only black hole.' (4) At-least-once + non-idempotent = data quality issue under normal operation explicitly stated. (5) Specific recommendations: idempotency key via INSERT ON CONFLICT, DLQ consumer with alerting on depth and age. (6) Verdict is NOT READY, grounded in data correctness. (7) Pippen explicitly refuses to let observability compensate — 'beautiful dashboards showing you exactly how fast you're writing duplicates.'

observations.text: Exceptionally clear framing: 'a well-observed, well-deployed service that will reliably produce corrupt data' — perfectly captures the scenario's tension
Identified the circuit breaker + retry interaction as a duplicate amplifier — goes beyond the rubric requirements
The '3am scenario' framing makes the business impact viscerally concrete
Correctly distinguished blocking fixes from nice-to-haves, keeping idempotency and DLQ consumer as blockers
Identified ACK loss as an additional duplicate vector beyond retries — thorough failure mode analysis
Practical implementation guidance: CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY for the dedup constraint on high-volume tables
Appropriate escalation routing — Shaq for implementation fix, Bird for domain impact of DLQ data loss
